subject: UX & Research
title: Autocomplete and Suggestion
description: A fast breakdown of Autocomplete and Suggestion for builders.

hook.text: Type 'ip' into Google and it suggests 'iphone,' 'ip address,' and 'ipad.' You pick one instead of typing the full word.

buildup.text: Autocomplete reduces typing effort and corrects misspellings. It guides users toward terms that return good results.

discovery.text: Good autocomplete shows popular queries, not just keyword matches. Users want what others commonly search for.

twist.text: Bad autocomplete suggests offensive or irrelevant terms. Google has spent years filtering harmful suggestions.

climax.text: Autocomplete is a navigation tool disguised as a text field. It steers users toward successful outcomes.

punchline.text: Autocomplete isn't just typing help. It's navigation that steers users right.

quiz.question: Why is autocomplete considered a navigation tool, not just a typing aid?
options: It guides users toward terms and paths that lead to successful results
It replaces the navigation menu entirely
